ew_context: Inverse H&S completion suggests end of corrective decline - likely Wave B or 2 completion with reversal targeting higher degree resistance
pattern_desc: Well-formed inverse head and shoulders with head at 202.77 and neckline at 203.33 - entry on neckline break confirmation
invalidation: Close below 202.77 on H1 timeframe breaks the head level and negates the reversal pattern
market_context: CHF strength amid Iran war tensions supports safe haven bid, while JPY weakness continues on BoJ dovish stance
full_analysis:
## Market Structure & Technical Context

CHFJPY has completed a textbook inverse head and shoulders pattern with the head formed at 202.77 and neckline resistance at 203.33. Current price at 203.29 sits just below the neckline, positioning for a breakout confirmation. The pattern spans multiple swing lows at 202.88, 202.77, and 202.97, creating the classic reversal formation. EMA alignment shows EMA20 (203.29) marginally above EMA50 (203.21), indicating early bullish momentum.

RSI at 60.4% demonstrates healthy upward momentum without overbought extremes, while ATR of 0.18 suggests controlled volatility. The 2.6x ATR stop loss at 202.83 provides adequate protection below the pattern's structural support.

## Key Levels

- **Resistance**: 203.33 (neckline breakout), 203.90 (measured move target)
- **Support**: 202.83 (stop loss), 202.77 (head formation)

## Invalidation Level

Close below 202.77 on H1 timeframe would break the head level, invalidating the inverse head and shoulders pattern and suggesting continued bearish pressure.

## Macro & Flow Context

Geopolitical tensions favor CHF safe haven demand while BoJ's continued dovish stance weighs on JPY. CFD momentum alignment supports the technical reversal setup with laminar flow conditions.
